turns.user_message: I noticed lately that I just sit back and let other people make all the decisions for the group. It used to not bother me at all when I stayed quiet, but now it feels like I am just taking up space. I catch myself apologizing for breathing too loud or asking for things I actually need. It is weird how quickly my voice just disappears when I am around others. I keep wondering why I even bother trying to share my thoughts when they probably do not matter anyway.

This pattern keeps repeating itself no matter where I go or who I am with. I used to brush off my tendency to shrink away from attention, but now it feels like a heavy weight I drag around everywhere. Every single day I watch myself step aside and hand over my own needs to people who do not even ask for them. I tell myself it is just easier to stay out of the way, but deep down I know I am just hiding because I feel completely inadequate. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own presence and make myself smaller.

I honestly cannot stand looking at myself anymore because I feel like a complete ghost in my own life. What started as just staying quiet has turned into this overwhelming belief that I am fundamentally broken and unworthy of taking up any room. I watch myself completely erase my own opinions and just nod along, even when everything inside me is screaming to speak up. It hurts so much to realize I have convinced myself that my voice is worthless and that I deserve to be pushed to the sidelines. I am so tired of being the person who always fades into the background.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
